meta.name: PHQ-9 Patient Health Questionnaire
meta.description: The PHQ-9 is a brief, reliable self-report screening tool for depressive symptoms, consisting of 9 items corresponding to the 9 DSM-IV diagnostic criteria for major depressive episodes, used to assess the severity of depressive symptoms over the past two weeks.
meta.author: Kroenke, Spitzer & Williams (2001)
meta.reference: Kroenke K, Spitzer RL, Williams JBW. The PHQ-9: validity of a brief depression severity measure. J Gen Intern Med. 2001;16(9):606-613.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?

questions.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your depression score is within the normal range, suggesting no clinically significant depressive symptoms over the past two weeks. Occasional low mood is a normal part of life and does not indicate a problem.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle with regular exercise and balanced nutrition
Keep up regular social activities and connections with people you enjoy
Allow yourself time to relax and recharge when you feel occasional low mood

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: You may be experiencing mild depressive symptoms such as low mood or reduced interest that could slightly affect your daily functioning. These symptoms are common and often respond well to lifestyle changes and self-care strategies.
suggestions: Practice consistent self-care by maintaining a regular sleep schedule and daily exercise routine
Talk to a trusted friend or family member about how you have been feeling lately
Learn and practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing, mindfulness, or journaling
If symptoms persist beyond two weeks, consider scheduling a visit with a healthcare professional

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are at a moderate level and are likely affecting your work performance, social relationships, and daily activities. Professional evaluation is recommended to determine whether treatment would be beneficial.
suggestions: Consider scheduling an appointment with a counselor, therapist, or psychiatrist for professional evaluation
Maintain a structured daily routine including regular wake times, meals, and physical activity
Engage in physical exercise at least three to four times per week, as it has proven antidepressant effects
Avoid using alcohol or other substances as coping strategies, as they can worsen depression
Explore evidence-based psychotherapy options such as cognitive behavioral therapy or behavioral activation

ranges.label: Moderately Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are at a moderately severe level and are likely significantly impairing your daily functioning and quality of life. Seeking professional mental health support is strongly recommended at this stage.
suggestions: Seeking evaluation from a psychiatrist or mental health professional is strongly recommended at this severity level
A combination of medication and psychotherapy is often the most effective treatment for this level of depression
Make sure you have a trusted person you can reach out to when you are struggling
Stay connected with others and avoid isolating yourself, even when motivation is low

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your depressive symptoms are very severe and are likely seriously affecting all areas of your daily life. Immediate professional help is essential, and if you have thoughts of self-harm, please reach out for crisis support right away.
suggestions: Please contact a psychiatrist or visit a hospital emergency department as soon as possible for urgent evaluation
If you are having thoughts of self-harm or suicide, please call the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline (US) or 400-161-9995 (China) immediately
Do not stay alone; inform a trusted family member or friend about your current situation right away
Follow your treatment recommendations closely, including taking medications exactly as prescribed
Remember that severe depression is a treatable medical condition and recovery is possible with proper support
